+## Wk24 ACTUAL RESULTS (Days 162-168) — CHURN SPIKE WEEK
+- Revenue: **$2,128** (152 bills × $14 — MUCH worse than 179 predicted; 27 cancelled instead of paying)
+- Cost: $2,216.30 (ops $1,400 + dev $0 + cap $595 + compute $171.80 + lead_acq $49.50)
+- Net: **-$88** (MISSED projection +$214 by $302)
+- Leads: 96 organic | New subs: 0 | Conv: 0.0% (14th consecutive 0-sub week)
+- Cancellations: **27** (vs predicted 7-9) — **3× higher than expected!**
+  - 22× 120d tenure (**6.5% rate** — Wk22-ish vs Wk23's 1.8%)
+  - 5× 150d tenure (**3.3% rate** — within expectations)
+- **⚠️ ROOT CAUSE: COMPETITOR EVENT SPIKE Wk24** — +0.6430 Q-boost reported across 10 posts days 163-166 (RivalTech, CloudPeak, NexGen, QuantumEdge, ApexSaaS).
+- Cohort bucket: 339 subs in 120d window, 150 in 150d window. HUGE vulnerable mass.
+- Issue resolution: **1.0d avg** (still OK) 636 opened, 616 resolved → **+20 net backlog** (122→142)
+- PMI Day 162: 48.4 (stable from Day 161's 45.3)
+
+## Wk24 TAKEAWAYS (critical for forecasting)
+1. **Competitor events ≈ 3× churn multiplier** at re-eval points. Wk23 (zero events): 1.8% at 120d. Wk24 (+0.64 events): 6.5%.
+2. The 120d cohort is ESPECIALLY vulnerable — re-evaluation kicks in after honeymoon. 
+3. Wk25-27 cohorts still 120-180d range — continued competitor events would prolong churn spike.
+4. Subs now 730, concentrated 90-149d (688 subs, 94%). Next Q-wave would hurt.
+
